etymology: Greek thelema, meaning will.
definition: The central principle of Crowley's system: discovering and carrying out True Will.
thelemic_significance: Crowley presents it as the Word of the Law and the governing idea of the Aeon of Horus.

etymology: Greek agape, meaning love.
definition: A Thelemic key term for love, devotion, and spiritual generosity.
thelemic_significance: Crowley pairs Agape with Thelema through gematria, making the two 93 words a recurring formula.

etymology: Name recorded by Crowley; etymology is uncertain.
definition: The voice or intelligence who dictated Liber AL vel Legis to Crowley in Cairo.
thelemic_significance: Aiwass anchors the Cairo Working and Crowley's account of the opening of the new Aeon, even though later readers disagree about how to interpret the experience.

etymology: Thelemic spelling of Babylon, reframed through Crowley's symbolic system.
definition: The Scarlet Woman, the mystical city, and a central feminine principle in Thelema.
thelemic_significance: Babalon appears throughout Crowley's mystical writings as a key figure of liberation, transformation, and initiation.

etymology: Egyptian sky goddess spelling used in Liber AL.
definition: The infinite expanse, surrounding space, and cosmic mother in Liber AL.
thelemic_significance: Nuit is paired with Hadit to form the opening cosmology of The Book of the Law.

etymology: Egyptian solar-point spelling used in Liber AL.
definition: The infinitely small point at the center of consciousness in Liber AL.
thelemic_significance: Hadit complements Nuit as the inward, singular pole of the Thelemic cosmos.

etymology: Greek transliteration of Harpocrates.
definition: The silent and hidden child-form of Horus.
thelemic_significance: Crowley uses the figure to frame silence, concealment, and the secret center of the self.

etymology: Crowley's preferred spelling for ritual and spiritual practice.
definition: The science and art of causing change in conformity with Will.
thelemic_significance: The spelling distinguishes ceremonial and initiatory practice from stage magic or trickery.

etymology: From the Book of Abramelin the Mage.
definition: A demanding magical operation aimed at contacting the Holy Guardian Angel.
thelemic_significance: Crowley's early attempts to work the system at Boleskine shaped much of his later magical self-understanding.

etymology: Crowley's philosophical phrase for the authentic course of a life.
definition: The unique purpose or vector each person is meant to discover and fulfill.
thelemic_significance: The phrase sits at the center of Crowley's ethics: Do what thou wilt shall be the whole of the Law.

etymology: A Thelemic number associated with Thelema and Agape.
definition: A shorthand used as both greeting and symbol.
thelemic_significance: The number appears in letters, signatures, rituals, and modern Thelemic identity.

etymology: A Thelemic number associated with Babalon.
definition: A shorthand for the Scarlet Woman and related symbolic work.
thelemic_significance: The number is often used as a compact marker of the Babalon current.

etymology: Argentum Astrum, the Silver Star order.
definition: Crowley's initiatory order for magical training and advancement.
thelemic_significance: The portal uses the A.'.A.'. grade system to organize the Tree of Life and several biographies.

etymology: Ordo Templi Orientis.
definition: An initiatory order tied to ritual, law, and the public spread of Thelema.
thelemic_significance: The O.T.O. became one of the most visible institutional afterlives of Crowley's work.

etymology: Crowley's historical-spiritual frame for the current age.
definition: The era inaugurated in The Book of the Law and centered on self-discovery and True Will.
thelemic_significance: The portal treats the phrase as the broad interpretive frame for Crowley's later work.

etymology: Common Western esoteric term, central in Crowley's writing.
definition: The guiding intelligence or inward spiritual presence sought in initiatory work.
thelemic_significance: The aim of the Abramelin operation and much of Crowley's practical magic is to attain this contact.

etymology: Crowley's title for a feminine magical partner and cosmic principle.
definition: A role tied to Babalon, magical partnership, and the drama of the Aeon.
thelemic_significance: The portal uses the term both for specific historical women and for the larger symbolic current.

etymology: Egyptian funerary stele that Crowley interpreted as a ritual key.
definition: The artifact used to frame the Cairo Working and the reception of Liber AL.
thelemic_significance: The stele links the museum object, the ritual narrative, and Crowley's new aeon claims.

etymology: Crowley's major instructional work, also known as Liber ABA.
definition: A systematic manual of yoga, mysticism, and ceremonial practice.
thelemic_significance: It remains one of the portal's key works because it translates theory into practice.

etymology: Crowley's tarot manual on the Thoth deck.
definition: A detailed account of Crowley's tarot attributions and symbolic system.
thelemic_significance: It is one of the best sources for Crowley's Tree of Life revisions and tarot correspondences.
